title: Swarm CLI + Tauri IPC bridge for harness invocation
goal: Expose the swarm harness via a CLI binary `handshake-swarm` AND a Tauri IPC `kernel.swarm.run(scenario_id, n)` so the Diagnostics panel (cluster X) can launch a swarm scenario interactively. CLI is for CI; IPC is for in-app operator triggering.
expected_diff_shape: New bin target src/backend/handshake_core/src/bin/handshake-swarm.rs in Cargo.toml; Tauri command in app/src-tauri/src/swarm.rs; ScenarioRegistry trait + builtin scenarios (the N=8 perf, a session-cancel scenario, a lease-contention scenario).
unblock_conditions: MT-034 harness primitive ships; MT-007 ProcessLedger ships (swarm CLI run registers itself as a process).
implementation_notes: Two exposure paths over the same MT-034 primitive. CLI bin: `handshake-swarm --scenario <id> --n <count> [--report <path>]` runs the named scenario and writes a SwarmReport JSON to the optional report path or stdout. Scenarios registered via `ScenarioRegistry::register("n8-perf", N8PerfScenario)` etc.; this MT ships three built-in scenarios: `n8-perf` (alias for MT-035's scenario), `lease-contention` (10 sessions racing on one lease), `cancel-mid-mutation` (8 sessions, half cancel after first mutation). Cargo.toml: add `[[bin]] name = "handshake-swarm" path = "src/bin/handshake-swarm.rs"`. Tauri IPC: `#[tauri::command] async fn swarm_run(scenario_id: String, n: usize) -> Result<SwarmReport, String>` — registers the swarm run in ProcessLedger with `engine_kind="swarm_harness"`, drives the harness from MT-034, returns SwarmReport. The IPC must be gated behind a debug-build feature `swarm_ipc` so it does NOT ship in release builds (per the spirit of MT-029 inspector feature gate — swarm-from-UI is a debug-only surface). Per HBR-MAN-001 the new Tauri command + CLI flag set must update ModelManual in the same commit (MT-013 manual content adds entries for kernel.swarm.run, handshake-swarm CLI, and all three scenario ids). Tests: invoke CLI with `n8-perf --n 2` (small N for unit-test speed), assert SwarmReport JSON parses; invoke Tauri IPC mock, assert SwarmReport returned.
risk_if_missed: Without CLI/IPC the swarm harness is only reachable from cargo tests; ops cannot run ad-hoc swarm scenarios during diagnosis.
validator_focus: ScenarioRegistry is extensible (test asserts a custom scenario can be registered); CLI and IPC drive identical harness code path (no duplicated orchestration); ModelManual entries exist for all new surfaces (HBR-MAN-001).

minimum_controls: ScenarioRegistry extensibility tested (a custom scenario can be registered without touching MT-034).
CLI and IPC share orchestration code path (single source).
swarm_ipc is feature-gated to debug builds only.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: handshake-swarm CLI bin and kernel.swarm.run Tauri IPC both call run_swarm_scenario(scenario_id, n) from the same scenarios.rs registry; CLI is not feature-gated (callable in CI) and IPC is double-gated by debug_assertions AND feature "swarm_ipc"; the ScenarioRegistry accepts custom scenarios per the extensibility red-team control.
